User and guest access management in Shale.

Shale gives you fine-grained control over who can view, annotate, and approve content in a review session.

## User roles

| Role | Permissions |
|------|-------------|
| **Owner** | Full admin access, billing, org settings |
| **Admin** | Manage users, create sessions, approve |
| **Reviewer** | View sessions, add annotations and comments |
| **Guest** | View and comment via timed link only |

## Inviting users

Go to your organization settings and invite team members by email. They'll receive an invitation to create a Shale account linked to your organization.

## Guest links

For external stakeholders who don't need a full account, generate a **timed guest link** from within any session. Links can be set to expire after a defined period.

## Approver hierarchy

Shale supports structured approval chains — define which roles must approve before a session is considered signed off. All approvals are logged for chain of custody tracking.

## User logging

All session activity (views, annotations, approvals) is logged per user for audit and accountability purposes.

Sketch-style feedback tools for annotating 3D environments.

The Shale brush engine lets reviewers draw directly on 3D environments with expressive, layered visual notes.

## Drawing tools

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Brush** | Smooth, pressure-sensitive strokes for freeform markup |
| **Pencil** | Hard-edged strokes for precise linework |
| **Eraser** | Remove strokes selectively |

## Stroke properties

- **Size** — adjust brush radius
- **Opacity** — control stroke transparency
- **Hardness / Softness** — feather or sharpen stroke edges
- **Color** — integrated color picker for any markup color

## Editing strokes

Existing strokes can be **panned**, **rotated**, and **scaled** after placement for precise positioning.

## Layered annotations

Multiple reviewers can leave independent annotation layers on the same scene. Layers can be toggled on/off to review feedback individually or all at once.

Context-aware comments tied to scene location and camera view.

Shale's comment system ties written feedback directly to a position and camera angle in the scene, so reviewers can always see exactly what a note refers to.

## Creating a comment

Click anywhere in the review environment to place a spatial note. Notes can contain:

- Written text
- Drawn annotations (via the brush engine)
- Image references
- 3D model references

## Go-to context view

Every comment stores the **exact camera position** at the time it was created. Click **Go To** on any note to jump back to that view instantly — no hunting around the scene.

## Comment threads

Comments support threaded replies, so conversations stay attached to the relevant scene location rather than drifting into external chat tools.

## Approval states

Comments can be marked with approval states (e.g. **Open**, **In Progress**, **Resolved**, **Approved**) to track progress through a review cycle.

What Shale is and how it fits into your 3D workflow.

Shale is a review and approval solution designed for real-time collaboration on 3D content across industries like game design, architecture, VFX, and construction. Speeding up creative cycles by getting notes, feedback and annotations getting directly back in the hands of the artists in real-time. ## Who is Shale for? * Anyone creating, sharing and looking for feedback on shared documents! * **Game studios** — review levels, assets, and environments with your team and clients * **Architecture firms** — walk through models and collect structured feedback from stakeholders * **VFX teams** — annotate renders and 3D scenes with context-aware notes tied to camera view * **Interior Design** — share notes on photos with contractors and give clients a great way to see their new decor ## How it works 1. Login to the Shale Portal 2. Create an organization and invite collaborators 3. Create a project and either upload existing content for review OR open your preferred compatible DCC 4. Install the Shale Plugin in your DCC and login 5. Create your content 6. Mark the area you want reviewed and export to the Shale App 7. Send reviewers a link to your Shale project on the app, or choose a project and asset to open and review 8. Reviewers annotate directly on a representation of the 3D environment using the brush engine and comment tools 9. Feedback flows back into your DCC as embedded comment threads with approval states 10. Iterate, resolve, and track the full chain of custody through to final sign-off ## Platforms Shale runs on **Web**, **Desktop**, and is optimized for **iPad and tablets**. ## **Image Example** ![ScreenShot Example](/images/environment.jpg) ## **Video Example**

Get up and running with Shale in minutes.

## 1. Install the plugin

Download and install the Shale plugin for your DCC tool:

- [Unreal Engine](/integrations/unreal-engine/)
- [Blender](/integrations/blender/)
- [SketchUp](/integrations/sketchup/)
- [Maya](/integrations/maya/)

## 2. Create an account

Sign up at [shalereview.com](https://shalereview.com) and create your organization.

## 3. Start a review session

Open your scene in your DCC tool, then launch Shale from the plugin panel. Click **New Session** to create a shareable review environment.

## 4. Invite reviewers

Share the session link directly, or generate a timed guest link for external stakeholders who don't need a full account.

## 5. Collect feedback

Reviewers annotate the 3D environment using the brush engine and comment tools. All notes are tied to scene location and camera view, and sync back to your DCC automatically.

The Shale add-on for Blender integrates the review pipeline directly into your modeling and rendering workflow.

## Requirements

- Blender 3.6 or later
- Shale account with an active organization

## Installation

1. Download the Shale add-on (`.zip`) from the [Shale dashboard](https://shalereview.com)
2. In Blender, go to Edit → Preferences → Add-ons → Install
3. Select the downloaded `.zip` and enable the **Shale** add-on

## Starting a review

Open the Shale panel in the N-panel (press `N` in the viewport), sign in, and click **New Session**.

The Shale plugin for Unreal Engine lets you launch review sessions directly from your UE project and receive annotated feedback as embedded comment threads.

## Requirements

- Unreal Engine 5.1 or later
- Shale account with an active organization

## Installation

1. Download the Shale plugin from the [Shale dashboard](https://shalereview.com)
2. Copy the plugin folder into your project's `Plugins/` directory
3. Open your project and enable **Shale** in Edit → Plugins
4. Restart the editor

## Starting a review

1. Open the Shale panel (Window → Shale)
2. Sign in with your Shale account
3. Click **New Session** to create a review environment from your current scene
4. Share the session link with reviewers

## Receiving feedback

Annotations and comments from reviewers appear in the Shale panel, tied to their original scene location and camera view. Click **Go To** on any note to jump directly to the reviewed position.
